review_metadata.j1_status: APPROVED 2026-05-16 — verdict not relitigated; this synthesis covers execution defects only

summary: Eight consolidated blocking findings; four are independent corroborations from all four reviewers (test-caller undercount, transaction-scope contradiction, missing table classifications, missing same-path validator). Two are independent single-reviewer findings of high consequence (raw_connection pool poisoning; schema-sentinel deletion). Architectural design (Option (c), L0/L3 split, MigrationRecorder Protocol pattern) is sound; defects are in the plan's execution scaffolding and grounding against current code.

severity_scale.3: Implementation fails to converge / blocks deploy

reversibility_scale.3: Irreversible without rework (binary fixtures, deleted code, shipped DDL)

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Test-caller undercount in Task 5a (9 listed, 51 actual)
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: grep -rln initialize_session_schema tests/ returns 48-51 unique files / 178 call sites at HEAD. Plan enumerates 9.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Delete the enumerated 9-file 'exhaustive' list from Task 5a. Replace with the grep directive only, and add a per-file routing-decision sub-step (caretaker fixture vs metadata.create_all). This commit must atomically update all callers per the No Legacy Code Policy.
consolidated_blocking_issues.blast_radius_note: Implementation will not converge at 9 files; CI breakage on the remaining 42 is the failure mode.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Transaction-scope contradiction (§3 single-transaction vs Task 4 raw_connection vs Task 7 per-table partial-failure record)
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Architecture and Systems both isolate the same internal contradiction; SQLite documentation corroborates non-transactional PRAGMA.
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: PRAGMA foreign_keys is connection-scoped and auto-commits; cannot be wrapped in a single transaction with the 12-step ceremony. MigrationFailedRecord vs MigrationPartialFailureRecord trigger conditions are not disjoint in plan text.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Replace §3 step 8 'single transaction' with 'per-action transactional discipline using the strongest available scope per action type.' Define MigrationFailedRecord (entire plan rolled back, no committed actions) vs MigrationPartialFailureRecord (at least one action committed before subsequent failure) with non-overlapping trigger conditions. Document which DDL operations in the 12-step ceremony are transactional vs PRAGMA-implicit-commit.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: engine.raw_connection() poisons SQLAlchemy connection pool with FK=OFF on ceremony error path
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Single reviewer (Architecture); Tier-1 audit-integrity consequence; concrete mechanism with SQLAlchemy pool semantics.
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: engine.py:69-81 connect event fires only on new DBAPI connections, not on pool checkouts. Connection returned to pool after step-2-11 exception still has foreign_keys=OFF. Subsequent checkouts silently skip FK enforcement.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Wrap shadow-copy ceremony in try/finally: in the finally block, either (a) call raw_conn.detach() to force-discard the connection from the pool before re-raising, or (b) restore PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON before connection returns to pool. Add post-ceremony assertion: acquire a new pooled connection and assert PRAGMA foreign_keys = 1; crash if not. Convert silent Tier-1 integrity failure into immediate detectable crash.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Schema-sentinel system silently deleted (application-identity guard + schema-epoch watermark)
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Architecture and Systems independently identify this; Systems names the archetype (fixes-that-fail).
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: schema.py:109-168: _stamp_schema_sentinels writes PRAGMA application_id (0x454C5350) and PRAGMA user_version (SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH=7); _assert_schema_sentinels rejects wrong-application SQLite files with 'This SQLite file does not belong to ELSPETH'. Neither function is mentioned in plan §§3-5 or Task 5a. OrphanLiveTable fires AFTER the caretaker has begun reflecting the target DB — too late to prevent operating on the wrong file.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Plan must explicitly resolve disposition. Recommended path: relocate _stamp_schema_sentinels into caretaker's fresh-DB code path; relocate _assert_schema_sentinels and call BEFORE reflection on existing DBs (or replace with equivalent application_id check). SESSION_SCHEMA_EPOCH may be retained as documentation constant. Update Task 5a to enumerate all three deletions/relocations.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: MigrationRecorder wiring strategy unspecified (per-call LandscapeDB.from_url vs held-open)
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Single reviewer (Systems); empirically verified that no long-lived LandscapeDB exists in app.state.
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: web/auth/audit.py and web/composer/tutorial_service.py both open LandscapeDB.from_url(...) as a context manager per-call. No app.state.landscape_engine in create_app(). For a 12-step shadow-copy ceremony emitting 2N+2 events per table, per-call pattern means 24+ open/close cycles on startup-blocking synchronous path.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Task 3 or Task 5 must explicitly choose: (a) MigrationRecorder holds URL+passphrase and opens per-write (consistent with web subsystem) OR (b) MigrationRecorder holds an open LandscapeDB for caretaker lifetime (efficient but architecturally divergent). Document the choice with comment explaining the trade-off.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Missing same-path model_validator on WebSettings (asserted in plan as existing)
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Single reviewer (Quality); confirmed not present in config.py.
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: §3 asserts (present tense) that a @model_validator(mode='after') rejects landscape_url == session_db_url. config.py has model_validators at lines 371, 387, 432, 447, 462, 472 but none check path equivalence. Task 5's integration test for this guard would test a non-existent validator and produce a false-confidence pass.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Add a sub-task (or expand Task 5) to: (a) implement _reject_same_path_for_landscape_and_sessions @model_validator(mode='after') in WebSettings; (b) add unit test in tests/unit/web/test_config.py for direct rejection; (c) ensure resolution accounts for default paths that share data_dir.

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Phase 6 tables missing from §2 classification (composer_completion_events_table, skill_markdown_history_table)
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Reality and Systems agree; 15 tables in models.py, 13 in plan.
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: models.py lines 719 (composer_completion_events_table, Phase 6) and 798 (skill_markdown_history_table) — both live in RC5.2. Phase 6 not listed as BLOCKED-on dependency in §8. CI gate (enforce_migration_classification.py) would catch at PR time; absent CI gate, caretaker defaults both to 'data' and emits classification-default warnings on every deploy as permanent audit noise.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Add both tables to §2 classification table with explicit classes (likely both 'data' — completion-event audit trail has immutability triggers per schema.py:38-40). Add Phase 6 to §8 BLOCKED-on list alongside Phase 1A and Phase 5b. Update Task 1 narrative to cover 15 tables. Update Task 9 gen_1 fixture description to acknowledge full schema-at-revision rather than abbreviated 4-table list (RR-009 ties in here).

consolidated_blocking_issues.title: Five caretaker refusal paths have no Landscape event types or record-then-raise contract
consolidated_blocking_issues.consensus: Single reviewer (Quality); audit-primacy is a Tier-1 invariant per CLAUDE.md ('if it's not recorded, it didn't happen').
consolidated_blocking_issues.evidence: MigrationUnsupportedDialectRefusal is correctly specified for UnsupportedDialect. The remaining five refusal paths — OrphanLiveTable/OrphanLiveColumn (§3 step 5), MigrationLockHeld (Task 7a), OrphanMigrationPlanRefusal (Task 7), NonEmptyDefinitionalTable (Task 4), DestructiveMigrationRefused (Task 4) — have no event type in Task 3 contracts and no TDD step asserting emit-before-raise.
consolidated_blocking_issues.resolution: Add named event types to contracts/migration.py: MigrationOrphanTableRefusal, MigrationOrphanColumnRefusal, MigrationLockHeldEvent, MigrationOrphanPlanRefusalEvent, MigrationNonEmptyDefinitionalRefusal, MigrationDestructiveChangeRefusal. Add record-then-raise contract to each execution path with TDD assertion. Cross-reference SYS-009 for error-path ordering question.

consolidated_warnings.title: Stale line numbers throughout plan (app.py:441 wrong; schema.py:226 and line 60 wrong)
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Correct to app.py:581 (call site, not FastAPI constructor); schema.py:75 (initialize_session_schema definition), :185 (call site for _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry), :377 (definition of _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry). Plan was authored against a stale snapshot — see pattern note below.

consolidated_warnings.title: Sub-phase split (9A/9B/9C) recommended; deferred to operator in §862
consolidated_warnings.resolution: OPERATOR DECISION. Architecture's recommended split: 9A = Task 1 + Task 3 (dataclasses) + CI gate (no runtime change, no one-way doors). 9B = Tasks 2, 4, 4.5, 5, 5a, 6, 7, 7a (atomic: Task 5 wires caretaker, 5a deletes initialize_session_schema — these MUST land together). 9C = Tasks 8-11 (verification, runbook, fixture CI). Rationale: 9A has zero runtime risk; 9B is the one-way door; 9C is documentation + observability. The 51-file caller sweep belongs in 9B.

consolidated_warnings.title: Six required SQLite triggers (_REQUIRED_SQLITE_TRIGGERS) registered via SQLAlchemy event.listens_for; will NOT fire on executor's direct CreateTable() in shadow-copy step 2
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Caretaker post-run validation must call _validate_required_triggers in addition to _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry. Shadow-copy ceremony must explicitly recreate registered DDL event listeners (or call Table.create(engine) which DOES fire DDL events — but verify interaction with raw DBAPI connection).

consolidated_warnings.title: Mid-SIGKILL recovery: orphan-plan refusal fires BEFORE OrphanLiveTable; runbook does not address second refusal in recovery sequence
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add step 3.5 to orphan-plan-record runbook: 'After clearing orphan plan, inspect live DB for <table>_new pattern; if present, follow OrphanLiveTable runbook entry before retry.' Alternatively, teach caretaker to scan all blocking conditions before any refusal and report them together.

consolidated_warnings.title: Tracer-bullet opportunity: 11 tasks sequentially before first end-to-end activation
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Insert Task 4.5: no-op caretaker that classifies + emits MigrationPlanRecord only (no DDL), wired into create_app(). Validates Protocol surface and activation timing before executor is implemented. Reduces blast radius if MigrationRecorder Protocol or synchronous startup hook needs redesign.

consolidated_warnings.title: CI scripts claimed in plan prose but not delivered by any task (enforce_migration_classification.py, enforce_migration_raw_sql.py, synthetic-only + SHA-256 fixture gate)
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Task 1 must explicitly ship scripts/cicd/enforce_migration_classification.py (not only in-test assertion). Task 4 must ship scripts/cicd/enforce_migration_raw_sql.py OR change prose to acknowledge code-review-only enforcement. Task 11 must cover synthetic-only domain pattern check AND SHA-256 tamper-detection — currently covers only schema-delta freshness.

consolidated_warnings.title: Three TDD steps specify what to test but not mechanism (Task 6 audit failure, Task 11 fixture sequencing, Task 4 raw_connection leak)
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Task 6: specify use of MigrationRecorder test-double raising LandscapeWriteError. Task 11: document two-step sequencing — fixture-freshness test first parametrized over placeholder, then updated after Task 9 fixtures land. Task 4: add connection-leak test asserting raw_conn returned to pool on exception (or detached) via engine.pool.checkedout() check.

consolidated_warnings.title: 12-step shadow-copy ceremony has no step-ordering assertion test
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add TDD step that captures emitted PRAGMA + DDL statement sequence via statement-capture fixture and asserts exact 12-step normative order. Outcome-only tests pass mis-ordered implementations.

consolidated_warnings.title: Force-clear-orphan-plan --reason text is Tier-3 operator input lacking validation (length cap, control-char sanitisation)
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Typer argument annotation with max_length=500, strip leading/trailing whitespace, reject ASCII control chars 0x00-0x1F (except tab/newline). Unit test in Task 7 TDD.

consolidated_warnings.title: Recovery loop dead-end if Landscape DB is itself the failure source
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add distinct runbook entry for 'Landscape DB unavailable/corrupt' (separate from sessions DB scenarios). Document diagnostic steps and acceptable recovery (Landscape backup restore, or — if confirmed unrecoverable — manual SQL clear with explicit audit-gap notation in incident record). Consider --force-without-audit escape flag with strong documentation.

consolidated_warnings.title: Error-path Landscape writes can swallow primary diagnostic (UnsupportedDialect, OrphanLiveTable)
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Consider reversing audit-primacy for ERROR-path records only: raise the actionable exception first, attempt Landscape write in finally/cleanup. The exception itself constitutes a record of sorts in stderr/process logs; the actionable message must always reach the operator. Document this as deliberate exception to the audit-primacy rule for error paths.

consolidated_warnings.title: Synthetic-fixture UUID constraint is unenforced — production and synthetic UUIDs are format-identical
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Either (a) use fixed synthetic UUID prefix (00000000-0000-0000-0000-XXXXXXXXXXXX) and CI gate checks prefix, or (b) add synthetic_fixture_metadata table to fixture DB with is_synthetic=1 boolean checked by CI gate. Document chosen mechanism in FIXTURES.md.

consolidated_warnings.title: Soft language for hard invariant: Task 5a says _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry test 'may need retargeting'
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Replace 'may need retargeting' with hard TDD step asserting run_caretaker calls _validate_partial_index_dialect_symmetry exactly once at end of execution. Use spy/mock for call-count and ordering assertion.

consolidated_warnings.title: Orphan plan-record runbook step (2) 'verify the sessions DB shape is consistent' has no mechanism
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Either add elspeth migration verify-shape <db> CLI subcommand alongside force-clear-orphan-plan (runs SQLAlchemy reflection, compares to metadata, emits structured consistency report), or document the exact SQL operator must run for each table. Runbook must give deterministic yes/no answers.

consolidated_warnings.title: Bootstrap ordering Landscape-schema-before-caretaker not declared as invariant
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add invariant statement to §3: 'Landscape schema is initialized before caretaker invocation; caretaker assumes structurally valid Landscape at startup.' Add TDD step in Task 6 asserting fresh-deploy (both DBs empty) correctly emits MigrationPlanRecord.

consolidated_warnings.title: Plan terminology mixes SQL table-name strings and Python variable names
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Choose canonical form for §2 classification table (SQL string names per metadata.tables.keys()) and add terminology note. Update Task 2 scenario language to match.

consolidated_warnings.title: ELSPETH_WEB__MIGRATION_DRY_RUN / MIGRATION_ORPHAN_SCAN_DAYS not declared on WebSettings
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add explicit sub-step in Task 3 or Task 5: 'Add migration_dry_run: bool = False and migration_orphan_scan_days: int = 90 fields to WebSettings in src/elspeth/web/config.py.' Without these declarations, env vars are accepted by Dynaconf but ignored by the application.

consolidated_warnings.title: Simplicity framing ('~400 LOC') understates total delivery surface
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Both reviewers agree this does NOT reverse the J1 verdict — Option (c)'s auditability advantage is the decisive factor. Correct framing only: update §2 trade-off Simplicity verdict to 'Medium — comparable total ceremony to Alembic; audit-native and additive-only coverage are the decisive advantages, not raw simplicity.'

consolidated_warnings.title: Binary fixture regeneration is prose-only, not a script
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add scripts/fixtures/generate_migration_fixtures.py with deterministic three-generation generation ceremony (checkout historical revision, boot app, seed synthetic data, copy file, register SHA-256). Phase 10+ regeneration is otherwise prose-only.

consolidated_warnings.title: Migration subcommand implementation file unspecified
consolidated_warnings.resolution: Add to Task 7: implementation location is src/elspeth/cli.py, following plugins_app pattern at lines 1230-1231 (typer.Typer + app.add_typer).

originals: A-003 (Architecture, demoted to warning by Architecture)

schema_sentinel_deletion.note: Severity-disagreement resolved upward per Systems' Tier-1 wrong-file-guard framing.

conflicts_resolved.architecture_view: Warning — A-007 names disposition as needing specification but does not flag wrong-file scenario
conflicts_resolved.systems_view: Blocking — SYS-002 identifies application_id guard as Tier-1 wrong-file protection independent of schema drift
conflicts_resolved.resolution: Promoted to BLOCKING per Systems' wrong-file analysis; Architecture's specification recommendation is incorporated as the resolution mechanism.
Held at BLOCKING per three-reviewer majority and empirically certain failure mode (CI fails at 42 unupdated files).

plan_strengths_acknowledged_by_reviewers: L0/L3 Protocol split for MigrationRecorder is layer-correct and aligned with CallRecorder/PluginAuditWriter pattern (Architecture verified)
Table.info['migration_class'] annotation is idiomatic SQLAlchemy and CI-gate enforcement is the right mechanism (Architecture verified)
Per-action audit granularity (5+ record types) is consistent with 'if it's not recorded, it didn't happen' audit primacy (Architecture verified)
RFC 8785 + SHA-256 canonical-JSON pattern matches contracts/composer_audit.py (Reality verified)
user_secrets non-negotiable 'data' classification + SELECT COUNT(*) guard is correct mitigation against misclassification (Architecture verified)
Plan explicitly hedges that its 9-file caller list 'should be confirmed by grep' — the verification step exists, only the 'exhaustive' framing is wrong (Architecture and Quality both noted this)
Plan explicitly defers sub-phase split, post-ship rollback, and fixture-generation approach to operator decision in §862 — these are documented decisions, not blind spots
post-ship rollback strategy explicitly documents the 'delete the DB' fallback as accepted cost (Architecture one-way-door review)
Memory entries (project_db_migration_policy, project_phase9_sqlite_only) match plan claims; SQLite-only scope is consistently maintained (Reality verified)

pattern_diagnosis.recurring_finding_across_cycles: Test-caller undercount and stale line numbers were also caught in Cycle 1 (per task prompt). Convergence across cycles indicates the plan was authored against a stale codebase snapshot rather than refreshed at the start of each revision cycle.
pattern_diagnosis.recommended_remediation: Before Cycle 3 (or before Task 5a executes), introduce a 'ground-truth refresh' step in the plan's execution checklist: run a small set of grep commands at execution-start and assert plan-text matches reality. Better: move the load-bearing assertions (caller-list contents, line numbers of relocated symbols, table count) from prose into executable preconditions — e.g. a script that runs at Task 5a kickoff and refuses to proceed if grep count differs from expected.
systems_archetypes_named: fixes-that-fail (SYS-002): sentinel deletion solves crash-on-drift while losing adjacent wrong-file guard
shifting-the-burden (SYS-010): Alembic ceremony replaced by distributed infrastructure of comparable total cost
optimistic-ordering (SYS-005): refusal mechanisms designed as mutually exclusive but fire sequentially in real recovery

operator_decision_items.title: Adopt 9A/9B/9C sub-phase split
operator_decision_items.reviewer_recommendation: YES — Architecture (R-002) recommends; rationale is non-ambiguous; deferred in §862 per plan itself.
operator_decision_items.decision_input: 9A has zero runtime risk and ships the CI gate that prevents future regressions. 9B is the one-way door (caretaker live, initialize_session_schema deleted, 51 test files updated atomically). 9C is verification + documentation. Splitting reduces review burden per phase from 24.5 blast-radius-weighted points to ~6/14/4.

operator_decision_items.title: Fixture generation approach (historical-revision-checkout vs synthetic-from-MetaData)
operator_decision_items.reviewer_recommendation: Architecture recommends adding a regeneration script regardless of approach (R-008). Systems' SYS-008 prefers synthetic generation with explicit synthetic markers (UUID prefix or metadata table).
operator_decision_items.decision_input: Both approaches are workable. Historical-revision-checkout preserves real schema-at-time fidelity but requires git operations in CI. Synthetic-from-MetaData is reproducible from code alone but cannot capture schema-state mistakes from prior revisions. Recommend hybrid: synthetic fixtures with explicit is_synthetic markers, regeneration script in scripts/fixtures/.

operator_decision_items.title: Post-ship rollback strategy (runbook option (a) backup-and-delete vs option (b) shadow-copy reverse)
operator_decision_items.reviewer_recommendation: Architecture's one-way-door review confirms (a) is documented and acceptable; no reviewer pushed for (b).
operator_decision_items.decision_input: Backup-and-delete-the-DB is the project's documented policy (project_db_migration_policy memory). Option (b) is not required.

execution_order_recommendation: 1. Apply ground-truth refresh: re-grep all line numbers and caller lists named in plan; correct against HEAD.
2. Resolve CB-1, CB-7, CB-8 (low-effort plan-text fixes — caller list, missing tables, refusal event types).
3. Resolve CB-6, CW-17 (add same-path validator and migration env-var fields to WebSettings).
4. Resolve CB-2 (transaction-scope contradiction): rewrite §3 step 8, define MigrationFailedRecord vs MigrationPartialFailureRecord trigger conditions.
5. Resolve CB-3 (raw_connection pool poisoning): specify try/finally + raw_conn.detach() + post-ceremony FK assertion in Task 4.
6. Resolve CB-4 (schema-sentinel disposition): explicitly state stamp/assert function migration in Task 5a.
7. Resolve CB-5 (MigrationRecorder wiring): document chosen pattern in Task 3 or Task 5.

9. Address remaining warnings batch — CW-3 (trigger recreation), CW-4 (recovery runbook), CW-6 (CI scripts), CW-7/CW-8 (TDD mechanisms), CW-9 (reason validation), CW-10 (Landscape failure runbook), CW-11 (error-path ordering), CW-12 (synthetic fixture markers), CW-14 (verify-shape mechanism), CW-19 (regeneration script).
10. Resubmit for Cycle 3 review or proceed directly to execution per operator preference.
